question: How can I deal with exams?
expected_answer: Exams can be stressful. It’s okay if exams make your chest feel tight and your thoughts race. You’re not the only one feeling this pressure. Exams can shrink life down to grades and pressure but you're more than a test. Explore this Guided program on the Pressure of Exams for practical tips on how you can deal with them with less stress. Explore the breathing exercises for relief from your anxiety.

question: I’m scared about my future
expected_answer: That fear of the unknown can feel overwhelming. You're not alone in this; it's something almost everyone wrestles with quietly. Watch this short video on Dealing with uncertainty about the future. Then explore the podcast on Dealing with uncertainty to find fresh ways of dealing with this uncertainty.

question: I’m scared everything is changing too fast
expected_answer: Change can feel scary because it pulls you out of what’s familiar and safe. It’s okay to feel uneasy. Take one step at a time. Focus on what you can control, like your mindset and actions. Talk about your feelings, stay grounded in routines, and remind yourself, you’ve faced change before and grown. Watch this short video on feeling overwhelmed by our life coach Martha Van Dam. Then listen to this short podcast on the Power of Acceptance.

expected_answer: You feel what you feel because emotions are your brain’s way of making sense of the world and protecting you. question: How can I feel happier in my daily life?
expected_answer: A simple way to boost happiness today is to spend just 5 minutes doing something you genuinely enjoy. It could be listening to a favorite song, stepping outside to watch the sky, calling someone you care about, or savoring a quiet cup of tea. These small, mindful moments can shift your mood and bring calm and joy. Here are 3 tips to be happy. Listen to this Teen Talk conversation On Happiness.

question: Why am I so scared of dying?
expected_answer: Fear of death is natural; your brain is wired to protect you. But often, this fear is really about the unknown or unfinished parts of life. Gently bring yourself to the present. What can you do today that brings peace, meaning, or connection? That’s where calm begins. This video on overcoming anxiety can help calm your anxiety. Listen to this podcast to help overcome the fear of death.

expected_answer: It’s natural to feel this way, & yes, deeply caring makes the idea of loss painful. It shows how much someone means to you. Try to channel that love into the moments you share now. question: What does it mean to be mentally healthy?
expected_answer: Being mentally healthy means having emotional awareness, handling stress with resilience, and nurturing healthy thoughts and relationships. Everyone's journey looks different, but exploring your own mind is a powerful first step. You can learn more in the Introduction to Mental Health podcast. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm and gain clarity.

question: What if I’m afraid to feel better, like I’m betraying them?
expected_answer: This is a quiet fear many people carry. Healing can feel confusing, like you're letting go of the person you lost. But the truth is, love doesn’t fade when you begin to feel lighter. This video on Dealing with Grief explores how healing doesn’t mean forgetting, it means learning to carry love differently. Explore this Guided program on Fear to understand what is going on and to help you deal with these fears.

question: Why do I feel hurt?
expected_answer: We all get hurt by what people say or don’t say, or what people do, or don’t do. Words not said, promises broken, or moments missed can leave deep emotional marks. question: What should I do if my parents argue a lot?
expected_answer: It's quite normal to feel stressed or anxious if your parents are arguing. It's not your fault. Arguments among adults and in families are normal and they need to work through their differences. You need to look after yourself and find someone you can talk to. Journaling also helps. Listen to this mini podcast My parents argue a lot, for more insights.

question: Why do I hold a grudge?
expected_answer: When we get hurt, our mind remembers that. It’s trying to protect us. This is why we remember all our past hurts, so they do not repeat. But this process can also damage our relationships if we are not aware of it, and many of the hurts we feel come from our own unmet expectations. Listen to this mini podcast on Holding a grudge.

question: Why is it hard to say sorry?
expected_answer: Saying sorry is not easy, and we can feel it's a sign of weakness. We feel defensive and justify our opinion, or what we have done. Learning to say sorry easily, quickly, and in the right way is a great skill to learn and can defuse situations, make people respect us more and make it easier for others to say sorry. It can strengthen our relationships at home and at work. Listen to this mini podcast on how to say sorry.

question: I am feeling neglected
expected_answer: It is normal to need to feel seen, valued and loved. What you are missing is a sense of connection. That is just human. Sometimes people around us are too busy, or stressed to give us the attention we need. Begin by giving yourself the care and attention and self-love you need. Journaling, being outdoors, rest, music and calming breathing exercises from the app can all help. Listen to this mini-podcast on Feeling neglected. Needing attention.

question: I am worried about my test results
expected_answer: It is normal to worry about something in the future that may impact us negatively. It is our mind's way of trying to protect us. It is worth asking if we can do anything now to influence the outcome. If we can’t, then worrying about it isn’t going to change anything - but that doesn’t stop the worry. Try one of these Breathing exercises or listen to these Soundscapes to feel more calm. Talk to someone. Explore this Guided program on Dealing with Fear.

question: How does substance use affect mental illness?
expected_answer: Substance use can deepen emotional struggles and make it harder to cope. Watch the short videos Overcome a habit and Overcome unhelpful habits to reflect on your patterns. The podcast Social Media Addiction explores how our phone habits affect the brain and behavior. You can also explore the Addiction Guided program for lasting solutions.

question: What are the risks of mixing alcohol with other substances?
expected_answer: Mixing alcohol or vaping with other substances can confuse your body and mind—making the effects stronger, unpredictable, or even dangerous. The event Understanding and overcoming addiction explores how combinations can lead to serious health risks and faster dependence. The video Overcome unhelpful habits helps you spot when casual use starts crossing a line. You can also reflect with the blog Self-awareness can help prevent addiction to stay in control and make safer choices.

question: What is gaslighting?
expected_answer: Gaslighting happens when someone makes you doubt your own reality or feelings. It’s a form of emotional manipulation that creates confusion and self-doubt. Healing means reconnecting to your truth, trusting your perception again, and surrounding yourself with people who respect your experiences and validate your emotions. Listen to this Mini-podcast on Gaslighting. Use the online journal to write down what you are thinking and feeling.

question: I’ve been gaslighted
expected_answer: Gaslighting happens when someone makes you doubt your own reality or feelings. It’s a form of emotional manipulation that creates confusion and self-doubt. Healing means reconnecting to your truth, trusting your perception again, and surrounding yourself with people who respect your experiences and validate your emotions. Listen to this Mini-podcast on Gaslighting. Use the online journal to write down what you are thinking and feeling.

question: What is ghosting?
expected_answer: Ghosting is when someone suddenly ends communication without explanation. It can leave you feeling rejected or confused, but it often reflects the other person’s discomfort, not your worth. Healing starts with self-compassion and remembering you deserve honest, caring communication in your relationships. Let people go with grace. Move on and write your next chapter. Listen to this Mini-podcast on Ghosting.

question: How can I bounce back when everything feels too hard?
expected_answer: When things get tough, it’s normal to feel stuck or drained. But resilience doesn’t mean pretending you’re fine—it means taking one small step forward, even when you don’t feel ready. Every time you keep going, you grow stronger. Listen to the podcast Building Resilience to learn how to rebuild strength and stay steady during difficult times.

question: Why can’t I stop checking my phone all the time?
expected_answer: Social media is built to keep your attention—it’s not a lack of willpower. But constant scrolling can make you anxious or disconnected. Try setting small boundaries like screen-free mornings or using apps that track your time. Listen to the podcast Social Media Addiction to understand what really drives these habits and how to take back control.

question: How can I spend less time on my phone without feeling bored?
expected_answer: Phones fill every spare moment, so boredom feels uncomfortable at first. But real calm comes from letting yourself unplug. Try replacing screen time with things that make you feel alive—music, walking, journaling, or art. Listen to the podcast Dealing with Phone Addiction to explore why phones hook us and how to find balance again.

question: How do I stop stress from taking over my life?
expected_answer: Stress builds when you ignore small signs—like tension, headaches, or irritability. Start by naming what’s bothering you and taking breaks before burnout hits. You can’t eliminate stress, but you can manage how it affects you. Listen to the podcast Manage Your Stress. Live Longer for science-backed ways to stay calm and centered.

question: Why is saying sorry so hard for me?
expected_answer: Apologies can feel uncomfortable because they make us vulnerable. But saying sorry doesn’t mean losing power—it means showing care and maturity. It helps rebuild trust and brings peace inside you too. Listen to the podcast Saying Sorry to understand how to apologize with honesty and self-respect.

question: Mental health problems in sport
expected_answer: If you play any sport, you will face a variety of mental health challenges including performance anxiety, feeling others are better than you, and sadness when you don’t reach your goals. Listen to this TeenTalk on Mental Health Challenges Facing Young Athletes. It's a conversation with a young man who is both an athlete and a coach, and studying psychology himself.

question: I am Gay and am struggling with my identity.
expected_answer: It can be a challenging time. You may be confused about your sexual identity, or it's different from what you think others will easily accept - family, friends and society as a whole. For specific questions, ask on the community forum and a coach will answer. Begin with accepting yourself as you are - Listen to this podcast on Acceptance. Explore this Guided Program on Identity to understand how our identities form. Then explore the program on Having difficult conversations, to find the best way of sharing how you feel with others. It’s so important to do this.

question: I have a commitment problem in relationships.
expected_answer: Relationships can be confusing - and we may struggle to make a commitment, because we do not want to be tied down, or feel we may meet someone ‘better’ - who fulfills all our emotional needs. There is no ‘perfect person’ - all relationships need communication, kindness and work. Only when we feel emotionally safe with a person can we be vulnerable and have space to love, and grow. Watch this short video on How to have better relationships. Read this blog Looking for love. Explore this Guided program on Love.

question: How can I deal with a fear of the unknown?
expected_answer: Fear wants to keep us safe, and the unknown presents risks it does not know how to handle, so we can get anxious. We cannot solve a problem that has not yet occurred, so fear does not go away. The key lies in understanding the nature of fear - that it is a product of our thinking process, may not always be real and exaggerates the risk of bad things happening in the future. Watch this short video - Dealing with uncertainty. Explore this Guided program on The Nature of Fear. Listen to this meditation Releasing Fear.

question: How to deal with procrastination?
expected_answer: We have many things we should be doing: to improve our lives, or a new project, or doing our tax return - and we struggle to get started. There could be many reasons behind this. Maybe we haven’t allocated the time needed, or are afraid of failure, or find the task boring. Understanding the underlying reason can help us deal with it better. Take a tiny step - that is most important. Listen to this mini-podcast on Dealing with Change. Explore this guided program on Dealing with Fear.

question: How can I fall in love?
expected_answer: Finding someone to fall in love with and develop a close relationship with is one of our deepest needs. It is as much about being the right person, as it is finding the right person. Explore the Guided program on Love for more information.
